description: Multi-file React typed context: provider + guarded hook whose types must line up with a shared interface across 3 files. Interlocking generics frequently trip a weak model → exercises the auto-verify gate.
prompt: This is a strict-TypeScript React 18 project. Build a typed auth context across files in src/. (1) src/auth-types.ts already defines `User` and `AuthState` — read it, don't redefine. (2) Create src/AuthContext.tsx exporting: a context created with React.createContext<AuthState | null>(null); an `AuthProvider` component (props: { children: React.ReactNode }) that holds `user` state (User | null), exposes `login(user: User)` and `logout()`, and provides a fully-typed AuthState value; and a `useAuth(): AuthState` hook that reads the context and THROWS an Error if used outside the provider (so its return type is AuthState, never null). (3) Create src/Profile.tsx: a component that calls useAuth() and renders the user's name, or a 'Sign in' button that calls login with a sample user when there's no user. Everything must pass `npx tsc --noEmit` with zero errors.
